input.background: Assume $\hat{\theta}_1$ and $\hat{\theta}_2$ are the two estimators of parameter $\theta$. Suppose that $E\left(\hat{\theta}_1\right)=0.9 \theta, E\left(\hat{\theta}_2\right)=\theta, \operatorname{var}\left(\hat{\theta}_1\right)=2$, and $\operatorname{var}\left(\hat{\theta}_2\right)=3$.
input.question: We will later discuss how to establish a lower bound on the variance using an inequality known as the Cramér-Rao inequality.

output.answer: We now discuss some basic methods of parameter estimation.
